source_pr.title: tests: add sorted result for a test
source_pr.body:
### What problem does this PR solve?

Issue Number: close #47411

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

The test result is unstable. This PR adds the annotation `--sorted_result` to the line.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [x] No need to test
  > - [x] I checked and no code files have been changed.
  > <!-- Or your custom  "No need to test" reasons -->

symptoms: The test result is unstable. This PR adds the annotation `--sorted_result` to the line.
failure_signature: The test result is unstable. This PR adds the annotation `--sorted_result` to the line.
root_cause_explanation: This case is flaky because the query `select a from deci union all select b from deci;` does not guarantee a stable output order, but the integrationtest harness was checking results as an ordered list. Depending on execution details, the two rows can appear in different order and cause intermittent failures. The fix makes the assertion order-insensitive by adding the `--sorted_result` annotation for that statement in the `.test` file.
fix_pattern: For integrationtest cases where row order is not guaranteed (e.g., UNION/UNION ALL without ORDER BY), add `--sorted_result` to make result checking order-insensitive (or add an explicit ORDER BY when appropriate).

evidence: evidence: The test result is unstable. This PR adds the annotation `--sorted_result` to the line.
